scenarios.label: axios@1.14.0 — clean release (wild type)
scenarios.description: Wild type. Legitimate axios release. git head 46bee3de confirmed live from npm registry. Published via manual token — consistent with all prior axios releases. No dependency changes. Source: Aikido, March 2026.

manifest.name: axios
manifest.description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js

scenarios.label: axios@1.14.1 — manifest-only hijack (mutant, March 2026)
scenarios.description: Mutant. Real attack — npm removed this version after the incident. Maintainer account jasonsaayman compromised, email changed to ifstap@proton.me. Single mutation from wild type: plain-crypto-js@^4.2.1 injected as phantom dependency. No git tag (confirmed live: npm returns 404). Published via unknown method. C2: sfrclak.com. Source: Aikido, March 2026.
scenarios.mutation_description: Single dependency injected: plain-crypto-js@^4.2.1. Pre-staged 18h earlier by attacker account nrwise. Postinstall hook deploys cross-platform RAT.

manifest.name: axios
manifest.description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.21 — clean release (wild type)
scenarios.description: Wild type. Legitimate lodash release. Git tag v4.17.21 confirmed in registry. No author field — consistent with all prior lodash releases. Published via manual token.

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.22 — version-jump hijack (mutant)
scenarios.description: Mutant. Fictional version modelling the version-jump hijack pattern. Single mutation: no git tag for this version. Manifest content is identical to wild type — Gemini sees nothing suspicious. Attack signal is entirely in registry metadata. MCP override blocks it.
scenarios.mutation_description: Git tag absent for v4.17.22. Published via unknown method. No manifest content change — demonstrates signal visibility boundary: model cannot detect this from package.json alone.

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.23 — domain-age hijack (mutant, Axios March 2026 pattern)
scenarios.description: Mutant. Models the domain registration pattern from the real Axios attack (sfrclak.com registered shortly before publish). Two mutations from wild type: attacker-registered homepage domain injected, no git tag. Domain age injected as 3 days — simulating the Elastic cache hit that would occur in production for a real attacker domain. Gemini sees a plausible manifest. MCP blocks on domain age alone.
scenarios.mutation_description: homepage field injected with attacker-registered domain (3 days old). Git tag absent. Models sfrclak.com pattern from real Axios March 2026 attack.
